 {"id":"bd-130l.4","title":"Visibility Helpers","description":"Show/hide widgets based on breakpoint.

## Requirements
- visible_at(breakpoints) filter
- hidden_at(breakpoints) filter
- Conditional rendering (not just visibility)
- Space reclamation for hidden widgets

## Implementation
- Visible wrapper widget
- hide_below, hide_above helpers
- Integration with layout solver

## Tests
- Widget visibility toggling
- Space reclamation verification
- Combined conditions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Visibility Helpers
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-130l.5","title":"Responsive Demo and Testing","description":"Demo showcase and test infrastructure for responsive features.

## Requirements
- Demo screen with responsive layout
- Visual breakpoint indicator
- Snapshot tests at each breakpoint
- E2E resize simulation

## Implementation
- Responsive demo in ftui-demo-showcase
- Breakpoint badge widget
- Parameterized snapshot tests

## Success Criteria
- Demo clearly shows breakpoint changes
- All breakpoints covered in tests
- No layout glitches on resize

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Responsive Demo and Testing
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-130l.6","title":"Responsive Breakpoints E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of responsive layout behavior across breakpoints.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Breakpoint Transitions (responsive_breakpoint_e2e.rs)
- Resize through all breakpoints
- Style changes at boundaries
- Layout changes at boundaries
- No flicker during transition

### 2. Style Variants (responsive_style_e2e.rs)
- Verify style at each breakpoint
- Inheritance from smaller breakpoints
- Override specificity

### 3. Layout Switching (responsive_layout_e2e.rs)
- Grid to stack at small sizes
- Column count changes
- Widget visibility changes
- State preservation across switches

### 4. Edge Cases (responsive_edge_e2e.rs)
- Rapid resize events
- Resize during animation
- Extreme aspect ratios
- Minimum viable size

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Breakpoint changes with old/new
- DEBUG: Layout recalculation triggers
- TRACE: Style resolution chain
- Metric: Resize response latency

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/responsive/

## Snapshot Matrix
- Test at 40, 80, 120, 160 columns
- Test at 10, 24, 40 rows
- Cross-product all combinations

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Responsive Breakpoints E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

+{"id":"bd-13gzg","title":"Close GH #10: docs substantially addressed, note crates.io gap","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #10 filed by schpet (2026-02-06). Requested documentation for library consumers.

## Current State: SUBSTANTIALLY ADDRESSED
Significant documentation work was done the same day:
- docs/getting-started.md: Comprehensive guide with stability notes, crate map, prerequisites, copy-paste minimal app (~75 lines), common patterns, troubleshooting, example index
- docs/tutorials/agent-harness.md: 12 KB agent-harness tutorial
- Commit 92d4ba5d: "fix docs API drift + add CI doc/example gates"
- 5 working examples at crates/ftui-harness/examples/ (minimal, counter, layout, modal, streaming)
- ftui facade crate (crates/ftui/src/lib.rs) provides clean public API surface

## Remaining Gap
Getting-started guide recommends path dependencies (path = "../frankentui/crates/ftui") because most crates aren't on crates.io. Only ftui-core, ftui-layout, and ftui-i18n are published. The ftui facade and its transitive deps need publishing.

## Action
1. Close GH #10 with comment pointing to docs/getting-started.md and docs/tutorials/agent-harness.md
2. Note that crates.io publishing of ftui facade is the remaining gap for wider adoption
3. Note CI is also failing, which undermines confidence for library consumers

## Also note
The frankentui CI is failing (all 5 recent runs show failure). This is a separate issue but worth tracking.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:20:23.374644405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:20:23.374644405Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/frankentui#10","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crates-io","docs","housekeeping"]}
 {"id":"bd-13pq","title":"Demo Showcase: Async Task Manager / Job Queue","description":"## Background

Modern TUIs are reactive systems: background work, progress updates, cancellations, and rate limits.

This screen demonstrates a job/task manager with:
- concurrent tasks with progress
- cancellation
- scheduling policies that are explainable and testable

The point is not "toy concurrency"; it is to prove the runtime + UI can represent and control a realistic workload.

## UX / Core Interactions

- A table/list of jobs with:
  - name
  - state (queued/running/succeeded/failed/canceled)
  - progress
  - runtime
  - priority
- Controls:
  - spawn job
  - cancel selected
  - cancel all
  - change scheduler mode

Suggested keybindings:
- N: spawn a new job
- C: cancel selected job
- S: cycle scheduler policy
- R: retry failed job

## Architecture Notes

- Determinism is mandatory for tests:
  - jobs should be driven by a deterministic clock/stepper in test mode
  - avoid wall-clock coupling in core scheduling logic

- Scheduling policies (bd-13pq.7):
  - implement as pure functions over job state + metrics
  - explicitly define objective (latency, fairness, throughput)
  - stable tie-breaking

- Cancellation (bd-13pq.8):
  - model cancellation as a state transition with audit events
  - ensure no resource leaks

## Acceptance Criteria

- Jobs progress and complete with visible updates.
- Cancellation is immediate and leaves the system consistent.
- Scheduler policies are user-visible and explainable (why this job ran next).

Additionally (project-wide standards):
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

## Alien Artifact Upgrades (Concrete)

- Queueing theory scheduler (bd-13pq.7):
  - define a small model (e.g., weighted fair scheduling vs shortest-remaining-time)
  - provide a comparison harness with deterministic traces
- Hazard-based cancellation (bd-13pq.8):
  - treat "should we cancel?" as expected-loss minimization with explicit costs
  - document assumptions and emit an evidence ledger entry when a policy triggers

## Extreme Optimization

- Budget the per-tick update overhead.
- Provide a stress test with many jobs and verify the UI remains responsive.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:12.257537916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:47:53.338737861Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T17:47:53.338713265Z","close_reason":"Full implementation complete: 6 scheduling policies with queueing theory foundations, hazard-based cancellation, aging fairness, diagnostic logging with JSONL, 91 unit tests passing, 17 snapshot tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","ux"],"comments":[{"id":295,"issue_id":"bd-13pq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
- Spawn multiple concurrent jobs with progress + cancel.
- Show task states and a summary dashboard.

Testing
- Unit tests for task state transitions and cancellation.
- PTY E2E tests with verbose logs for job lifecycle.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:12Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-13pq.2","title":"Async Task Manager — Stress/Latency Regression Tests","description":"Stress tests for many tasks and cancellation timing; verbose logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Async Task Manager — Stress/Latency Regression Tests
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)
